output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can legally deduct, and how to write a demand letter that forces fast repayment.
content_outline: Landlord Withholding Security Deposit? Know Your Rights & State Deadlines
Is My Landlord Required to Return My Deposit? Legal Timelines Explained
What Can a Landlord Deduct From Your Security Deposit (and What They Can't)
Security Deposit Not Returned: How to Write a Dispute Letter That Works
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Give You Your Deposit Back
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14-30 days; missing deadlines can entitle tenants to statutory penalties
Landlords cannot withhold deposits for normal wear and tear, minor scuffs, or routine cleaning
A formal security deposit dispute letter is the fastest, most effective first step before escalating to small claims court
Documenting move-in/move-out condition and communicating in writing dramatically increases recovery odds
output.cta: Stop waiting. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes and force your landlord to return what's yours.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and who need clear, urgent guidance on recovery rights
